Module com.example.battleship
Package com.example.battleship.controller

Class CombatController

java.lang.Object
com.example.battleship.controller.CombatController
public class CombatController extends Object
Handles HU-2 (the human player firing at the machine's board) and HU-4 (the machine automatically firing back). Translates every resolved shot into the matching 3D feedback: a red X on a miss, or a WreckageDebris3D cluster on a hit -- via Ship3D.markHit(int), which already handles that.

Listens to both boards through the model's own BoardListener (an BoardListenerAdapter subclass per board), rather than relying only on the return value of Game.fireAtMachine(com.example.battleship.model.board.Position): that's what lets the machine's own shots (fired from scheduleNextMachineShot()) drive the same visual feedback path as the player's shots, with no duplicated code.

Also drives HU-5's autosave: every resolved shot writes the game to disk via SavedGameRepository (see autoSave()), and the save is deleted once the match actually ends.

    CombatController.HumanBoardListener
    Shots landing on the human's board -- fired by the machine.
    private class 
    CombatController.MachineBoardListener
    Shots landing on the machine's board -- fired by the human player.

    HU-5: writes the current game state to disk after every resolved shot, so "Continuar" on the main menu always has the freshest possible state to offer -- there's no separate "save" action to remember to use.

    Once a match is actually won or lost, there's nothing left to "continue" -- clear the save so the menu reflects that.

  • Field Details

    • MACHINE_SHOT_DELAY

      private static final javafx.util.Duration MACHINE_SHOT_DELAY
      Pause before each machine shot. Set high enough (well past the camera's own 1400ms pan) that the very first shot of the machine's turn never resolves -- and shows its hit/miss mark -- before the camera has actually finished traveling to the board being shot at.

    • game

      private final Game game

    • view

      private final BattlefieldView3D view

    • targetMarker

      private final HoverMarker3D targetMarker

      private Position selectedPosition
      The cell picked on the first click, waiting for a second click on the same cell to confirm.

  • Constructor Details

    • CombatController

      public CombatController(Game game, BattlefieldView3D view, Map<Ship,Ship3D> playerShipViews, Map<Ship,Ship3D> machineShipViews)
      Parameters:
      game - the game being played
      view - the 3D battlefield
      playerShipViews - the human's ships, mapped to their 3D shapes (from ShipPlacementController)
      machineShipViews - the machine's ships, mapped to their 3D shapes (from FleetViewBuilder)

  • Method Details

    • attachTo

      public void attachTo(javafx.scene.Node pickSurface)
      Wires this controller's mouse handling onto pickSurface (normally the BattlefieldView3D itself), taking over from whichever controller was handling input before.
      Parameters:
      pickSurface - the node to listen on

    • onCellClicked

      private void onCellClicked(javafx.scene.input.MouseEvent event)
      First click on a cell selects it (shows targetMarker there) without firing; a second click on that SAME cell confirms and fires. Clicking a different cell just moves the selection there instead of firing -- this is what stops an accidental shot from a click that was really meant to start dragging the camera, since a single stray click during a drag only ever selects, never fires.

    • afterShotResolved

      private void afterShotResolved()

    • scheduleNextMachineShot

      private void scheduleNextMachineShot()

    • autoSave

      private void autoSave()
      HU-5: writes the current game state to disk after every resolved shot, so "Continuar" on the main menu always has the freshest possible state to offer -- there's no separate "save" action to remember to use. A failure here is logged but never allowed to interrupt the match itself; losing the ability to save shouldn't mean losing the ability to keep playing.

    • deleteSaveOnGameOver

      private void deleteSaveOnGameOver()
      Once a match is actually won or lost, there's nothing left to "continue" -- clear the save so the menu reflects that.

    • focusCameraForCurrentTurn

      public void focusCameraForCurrentTurn()
      Points the camera at whichever board is currently being shot AT -- the opposite board from whoever's turn it is, not the same one. On the human's turn, the human is aiming at the machine's board, so that's what the camera should show (and vice versa on the machine's turn, so the player can watch the incoming shot land on their own board). Public so MainGameController can call it once, right when combat starts, to move the camera off the player's own board (where it sat during placement) and onto the machine's board for the human's first turn.

    • notifyTurnChanged

      private void notifyTurnChanged()

    • notifyGameOver

      private void notifyGameOver()
